# Dylan McDermott

> Meteorologist and geospatial data professional who builds automated geospatial and weather-data workflows that turn scientific observations into reliable maps, analyses, and operational products.

## Profile

- Location: West Des Moines, Iowa, USA
- Current role: Meteorologist - Weather and Catastrophe Analyst, Property & Liability Resource Bureau (2025-Present)
- Education: M.S. and B.S. in Meteorology, Iowa State University
- Contact: dylanddermott@gmail.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dylan-mcdermott-193b18174/

## Work Examples

- [PLRB - Meteorologist](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=plrb-weather-systems): Builds and maintains the automated Python and ArcPy workflows, geospatial products, claims-facing ArcGIS applications, cloud services, and APIs that keep weather and catastrophe evidence accurate in daily production.
- [Corteva - Research Associate](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=corteva-field-sensing): Co-developed the Smartstick field-sensing platform and built the Python and ArcPy workflow that processed its GPS-linked observations into quality-controlled, plot-level geospatial analyses. Compared selected results with drone imagery and LiDAR, co-built a Gold Standard weather station and an automated soil-gas system, and built and maintained infrared radiometers in a fleet of more than 200.
- [Master's Thesis - Iowa State University](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=land-use-convective-weather): Designed and ran WRF and Noah-MP simulations to determine how historical land-use change affected Midwest rainfall and mesoscale convective systems.

## Core Capabilities

Meteorological analysis, catastrophe research, spatial analysis, Python automation, ArcPy, ArcGIS Enterprise, data integration, REST APIs, radar, numerical weather prediction, remote sensing, field sensing, drone imagery, LiDAR, WRF, Noah-MP, AWS, SQL, PostgreSQL, React, TypeScript, FastAPI, Git, GitHub, and Linux.
